title: Improving Lake Superior outflow regulation and quantifying uncertainty
reasoning: Lake Superior outflow regulation directly involves water-level monitoring and uncertainty quantification, which aligns with Hohonu's sensor/data services — but the academic/research framing and Great Lakes inland focus make this adjacent rather than core ICP. Michigan (MI) is a Tier 1 state, giving geography credit, and the open deadline makes it actionable, but the scope leans toward modeling/hydrology research rather than a sensor procurement or coastal flood monitoring deployment.
